seniority.level: Senior Leader / Head-of (likely Director or VP-level)
level_signals: Compensation range $240K-$325K base, consistent with Director/VP at a high-growth SaaS company
Expected to build RevOps infrastructure and scale the function
Prior experience with 50+ AE organizations required

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You've been in high-growth, high-velocity environments with 50+ AEs, ideally at companies that sell enterprise software at multi-$100K or $1M+ price points through direct outbound and partnerships.
employer_types_named: companies that sell enterprise software at multi-$100K or $1M+ price points

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You think about revenue from first principles. When something isn't working, you don't reach for an industry framework first: you ask why it's not working, build a hypothesis, and test it.
You have a bias toward iteration over perfection. You'd rather ship an imperfect analysis that moves a decision forward than spend two weeks on a polished deck that arrives too late.

You've used data, comp, and visibility to change behavior. You've redesigned incentive structures mid-cycle when they weren't driving the right outcomes, and you've been willing to make the case for uncomfortable changes.

You're a clear, direct communicator. Sales leaders and executives trust you because you tell them what you actually think, not what they want to hear. You can explain a nuanced revenue problem without burying the point.
Own pipeline and funnel analysis with a bias toward action. Identify anything broken, deeply understand it, propose a fix, ship it, learn from it, repeat.
Lead territory management as a living system. Continuously reassess coverage and help us read market signals to tweak our ICP and messaging.
Design and implement compensation plans that shape behavior. Align plans with company strategy, build buy-in, and deploy at scale.

Connect dots across marketing, finance, pre-sales, post-sales, and the exec team. RevOps at Hightouch is a vital connective function – your ability to surface information and collaborate across these groups will make you indispensable.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: ideally at companies that sell enterprise software at multi-$100K or $1M+ price points through direct outbound and partnerships.
employer_types_named: companies that sell enterprise software at multi-$100K or $1M+ price points

scope.team_size_estimate: 50+ account executives (GTM org size); RevOps team size not stated
scope.surface: Full revenue operations function: pipeline, forecasting, territory management, compensation, GTM analytics
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS, enterprise sales via direct outbound and partnerships
core_responsibilities: Drive fast, data-driven decision-making with sales leadership and the executive team
Own pipeline and funnel analysis with a bias toward action
Lead territory management as a living system
Design and implement compensation plans that shape behavior
Connect dots across marketing, finance, pre-sales, post-sales, and the exec team

compensation.equity_form: ISO options with early exercise and 10-year post-termination exercise window

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and highly competitive ($240K-$325K base)
Equity compensation includes early exercise and 10-year post-termination window — strongly candidate-friendly terms
Reports directly to CFO and CRO — high visibility and influence
Clear mandate with real ownership (not optimizing existing playbooks)
Company has strong customer traction (Domino's, Spotify, Chime, Ramp, 1000+ customers)
red_flags: E-Verify participation implies US work authorization is required; visa sponsorship status is not explicitly addressed
No explicit mention of team size under this role — unclear whether this is a team-building or individual-contributor-plus mandate
Fast-iteration culture ('days, not quarters') may indicate high-pressure operating cadence
Role appears to be greenfield RevOps infrastructure build — strong execution risk if support resources are not defined

The 'ideally at companies that sell enterprise software at multi-$100K or $1M+ price points' qualifier could be read as hard or soft; classified as nice-to-have (soft) because 'ideally' is a preference signal, while the core requirement of 50+ AE high-growth environment is treated as hard.
Whether this role manages a team is ambiguous — the JD says 'build the foundation for how RevOps scales' but does not mention direct reports or team headcount.
